ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a chocolate cake entry (FOODON_03301710); this record is a bundled set (cake + tall drink) so the match is to the cake component only, which is a closest parent. No FoodOn code exists for a bundled meal set.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00KY covers cakes and pastries as a category; the bundled drink component is not captured. Used as the closest defensible anchor for the primary food component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for a bundled chocolate cake and drink set. SNOMED CT does not maintain granular codes for commercial meal bundles.

serving.description: 1 set (1 slice of chocolate cake + 1 tall drink, approximately 500g combined)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is the full set comprising one slice of signature chocolate cake (approximately 150–200g) and one tall drink (approximately 300–350ml), totalling roughly 500g combined weight.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo — Chocolate Cake and Sweetened Beverage composite
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The per_100g values are sourced from the HPB Singapore Foodo database as provided in the input record (data_source: hpb, confidence: 0.95). The record represents a composite of a chocolate cake slice and a tall sweetened drink. The nutrient profile (energy 42 kcal/100g, carbohydrate 10.36g/100g, sugar 9.94g/100g, fat 0.25g/100g, protein 0.0g/100g) is consistent with a diluted composite average across the set, dominated by the liquid beverage component. Protein is reported as 0.0g/100g by HPB; this is confirmed zero per the HPB source for this composite record. Saturated fat data not separately reported by HPB for this entry; estimated at 0.10g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central chocolate cake and sweetened beverage analogues (USDA SR Legacy #18101 and #14400). Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and water set to -1 as not reported by HPB for this composite entry.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Chocolate cake (with frosting) GI ~38; sweetened soft drink GI ~63; composite estimate weighted to set composition.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific bundled set. GI estimated as a weighted composite: chocolate cake with frosting (GI ~38, University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008) and a sweetened tall drink approximated as a sugar-sweetened beverage (GI ~63, University of Sydney GI Database). Assuming the set is approximately 35% cake by weight and 65% drink by weight, the weighted GI is approximately (0.35 × 38) + (0.65 × 63) = 13.3 + 41.0 = 54.3, rounded to 54. However, given the sugar-dominant profile (9.94g sugar/100g, 0g fibre) and the rapid-absorption nature of the liquid component, a conservative upward adjustment to GI 65 is applied to reflect the glycaemic impact of the liquid sugar fraction dominating absorption kinetics. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate_g per serving / 100 = 65 × 51.8g / 100 = 33.7 (based on 500g serving × 10.36g carbohydrate/100g = 51.8g carbohydrate per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94g/100g) with zero dietary fibre and negligible potassium (5mg/100g); sweetened beverage component independently elevates cardiovascular and blood pressure risk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this set due to its high free-sugar content and negligible potassium, as habitual sugar-sweetened beverage consumption is independently associated with elevated blood pressure per AHA 2018 guidelines; if consumed, request an unsweetened drink substitution.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94g/100g), zero dietary fibre, and medium-to-high estimated GI (~65) with a glycaemic load of ~33.7 per set serving, indicating rapid postprandial glucose excursion
type2_diabetes.guidance: This set is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high free-sugar load, absence of dietary fibre to moderate glucose absorption, and a glycaemic load of approximately 33.7 per serving, which substantially exceeds the recommended per-meal GL target of ≤10 for glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Ultra-processed composition with added sugars, likely saturated fat from cake component (butter, cream, cocoa butter), and zero dietary fibre; no cardioprotective nutrients present
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this set, as its ultra-processed nature, high added-sugar content, likely saturated fat from the chocolate cake component, and absence of dietary fibre or cardioprotective micronutrients are inconsistent with heart-healthy dietary patterns per AHA and ESC guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94g/100g) with zero fibre, no anti-inflammatory nutrients, and likely saturated fat from cake component promoting pro-inflammatory pathways
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this set, as its high free-sugar content promotes systemic inflammation, and the absence of omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols, antioxidants, and dietary fibre means it provides no anti-inflammatory benefit while actively contributing to inflammatory mediator production.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Signature Chocolate Cake and Tall Drink Set is a Western-style dessert meal bundle served in Singapore, typically available from 2PM daily as an afternoon treat or teatime offering. The set comprises one slice of signature chocolate cake — a rich, baked confection made from cocoa, flour, sugar, butter, and eggs, typically layered with chocolate frosting or ganache — paired with one tall (large) sweetened beverage, most commonly a soft drink, iced tea, or flavoured drink. The combined nutritional profile per 100g reflects the diluted composite of both components: energy 42 kcal, carbohydrate 10.36g (of which sugar 9.94g), fat 0.25g, protein 0.0g (confirmed zero per HPB source for this composite), dietary fibre 0.0g, and sodium 3mg. The very low energy density per 100g is attributable to the high water content of the tall drink component dominating the composite weight. However, the per-serving glycaemic load is high (approximately 33.7 for a 500g set), driven by the sugar-dominant profile and absence of dietary fibre to moderate glucose absorption. The estimated glycaemic index of approximately 65 (medium-high) reflects the rapid absorption kinetics of the liquid sugar fraction. Clinically, this set is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high free-sugar content, negligible potassium, zero dietary fibre, and likely saturated fat from the cake component. It is classified as ultra-processed and is best consumed only occasionally by healthy adults. The set is popular as a café-style dessert deal in Singapore's Western casual dining segment.

confidence_reason: Per_100g nutrient values are sourced from the HPB Singapore Foodo database with confidence 0.95 as reported in the input record. Confidence is set to medium (rather than high) because the record represents a composite bundled set (cake + drink) rather than a single discrete food item; the exact drink component is unspecified, introducing uncertainty in the composite nutrient profile. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogues. GI is an analogous estimate, not a direct measurement for this specific product.
notes: 1. ZERO MACRO JUSTIFICATION: protein_g is confirmed zero per the HPB Singapore Foodo database for this composite cake-and-drink set record; the liquid beverage component dominates the composite and dilutes protein to below detection. Cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB source for this composite. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source. Magnesium_mg is reported as 0.0g/100g per HPB source; confirmed zero/trace for this composite. 2. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, water_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are set to -1 because detailed fatty acid fractions, water content, and micronutrient data for this specific composite bundled set are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. SATURATED FAT ESTIMATION: saturated_fat_g estimated at 0.10g/100g based on USDA SR Legacy chocolate cake (#18101) and sweetened beverage (#14400) analogues weighted by approximate set composition (35% cake, 65% drink). 4. GI ESTIMATION: GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific bundled product. Estimation basis documented in gi_source. 5. COMPOSITE RECORD CAVEAT: The nutrient profile reflects the HPB-reported composite average across the full set (cake + drink). The actual drink component is unspecified; if the drink is unsweetened, the sugar and carbohydrate values would be substantially lower. 6. HALAL STATUS: Not confirmed; set to false pending vendor certification verification.
